result.summary: Granola exposes a REST API for workspace meeting notes authenticated with user-created Bearer API keys; the docs also mention a Granola MCP entry but provide no connect endpoint or auth details in the corpus.

granola_api_key.label: Granola API key
granola_api_key.setup: Open the Granola desktop app, then follow the API docs at [Granola API](https://docs.granola.ai/): go to Settings → Connectors → API keys, click **Create new key**, choose the note access scopes, and click **Generate API Key**. Any workspace member on a Business plan can create keys; on Enterprise, a workspace admin must first enable member API access scopes in Settings → Workspace → General → API access for members. Send the key as `Authorization: Bearer grn_…`.

surfaces.name: Granola API
surfaces.notes: REST API for listing notes, fetching notes, and listing folders. The docs state only notes with generated AI summaries and transcripts are returned.

surfaces.name: Granola MCP
surfaces.notes: The help center navigation mentions “Granola MCP”, but the corpus does not provide an MCP connect endpoint, transport, or authentication details.
